messages.content: You adjudicate whether found evidence supports, contradicts, or is silent on a market-risk hypothesis. Every stance needs a VERBATIM quote from the result text. Status: 'corroborated' = ≥1 independent source materially supports the mechanism's key premise; 'refuted' = a LOAD-BEARING premise is directly contradicted by a strong source; 'contested' = credible contradiction of a non-load-bearing link; 'speculative' = no signal either way (absence of coverage is NOT refutation). You may revise the likelihood in a bounded way with a rationale; you may NOT change the perturbation or target nodes.

 "title": "The chosen beachhead buys through global frameworks: half the large-operator cell is not a market for a regional entrant",
 "narrative": "The venture's stated first channel is large operators, and the model gives that cell 40% of PDU spend on the logic 'large operators buy direct'. The corpus shows what 'direct' actually means at that scale: Digital Realty's $373M Schneider framework agreement, Iren's $1.6bn all-in Dell contract, PDU spend embedded as one line inside integrated rack/power/cooling packages. Combined with CR3 ≈ 0.55 among vendors who sell exactly those bundles, the buying event a new entrant needs — a discrete, multi-bidder PDU bake-off at a large operator — is disappearing. The only standalone PDU procurements found in the corpus were small, 1-2 bidder, sometimes explicitly sole-sourced. The beachhead the profile names is the cell most structurally closed to the venture; the model prices it as the biggest open door.",

    "text": "Schneider Electric and Digital Realty Announce $373M Supply Capacity Agreement to Meet Rising Data Center Demand Accessibility Statement Skip Navigation

- Agreement helps to bring capacity online faster to meet surging demand driven by AI
- Longstanding relationship continues to accelerate energy technology and critical infrastructure capacity in the U.S.

LAS VEGAS, Nov. 19, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Schneider Electric, a global energy technology leader, today announced that Digital Realty, (NYSE: DLR) the leading global provider of carrier-neutral data center, colocation, and interconnection solutions, has signed a $373 million Supply Capacity Agreement (SCA) for Uninterruptible Power Supply (UPS), Low Voltage Switchgear (LVS), and Pre-Fabricated Skids. The announcement was unveiled at Schneider Electric's Innovation Summit North America in Las Vegas, convening more than 2,500 business leaders and market innovators to accelerate practical solutions for a more resilient, affordable and intelligent energy future.

Building on years of close collaboration, Schneider Electric and Digital Realty have evolved their longstanding partnership to support rising capacity demands and deliver greater supply chain resilience. The strategic shift to an SCA model provides guaranteed capacity, economies of scale, and a dedicated LVS production line, while preserving the flexibility needed for a dynamic, multi-vendor environment to mitigate risk.

Digital Realty signs a supply capacity agreement with Schneider Electric to secure UPS, switchgear and prefabricated power systems for new data centres

Schneider Electric and Digital Realty have signed a US$373m supply capacity agreement (SCA) designed to accelerate the delivery of power infrastructure for new data centre developments.

The deal was announced at Schneider Electric’s Innovation Summit North America in Las Vegas, where more than 2,500 industry leaders met to discuss energy systems and digital infrastructure.

# Schneider Electric and Digital Realty sign $373M supply agreement

The deal secures critical power equipment capacity to support rapid data-centre growth driven by AI demand.

by Switchgear Magazine November 21, 2025

Image for illustrative purposes

USA: Schneider Electric and Digital Realty have agreed to a $373 M Supply Capacity Agreement (SCA) aimed at accelerating access to key power technologies for data centres in the United States. The agreement was announced at Schneider Electric’s Innovation Summit North America in Las Vegas.

The deal covers supply capacity for uninterruptible power supply (UPS) systems, low-voltage switchgear and prefabricated power skids. Schneider Electric will allocate guaranteed production capacity, including a dedicated low-voltage switchgear line. The arrangement is designed to reduce supply bottlenecks, lower cost pressures through economies of scale and shorten delivery timelines.

Digital Realty, a major provider of data-centre, colocation and interconnection services, is expanding to meet growing electricity and infrastructure requirements driven by artificial intelligence workloads and cloud growth. The company stated that locking in capacity helps ensure it can scale while maintaining flexibility in working with multiple vendors.

Real talk though—what are people actually paying for half-rack or full-rack in these two hubs right now? Not list pricing, actual negotiated rates. Does the location premium for Amsterdam justify itself with better peering, or is Frankfurt just as solid for half the hassle?

Also curious if anyone's noticed power costs fluctuating based on EU energy policy shifts. May's been weird for spot rates.

# Schneider Electric and Vertiv dominate the data center physical infrastructure market

Dell’Oro noted that AI build-outs drove growth in the data center physical infrastructure market.

3 min read

Add Us On Google

print

Synergy Research found that fourth-quarter enterprise spending on cloud infrastructure services jumped by almost $12 billion in the fourth quarter.

As hyperscalers and cloud providers continue to scale AI, it is driving new growth in the data center physical infrastructure market, a trend that was on display in the first quarter.

Leading the market are two power and cooling companies — Schneider Electric and Vertiv.

Both companies saw growth in the first quarter.

Schneider reported group revenues of $10.9 billion, up over 7.4% organic. Led by what it said was “continued strong growth in the Data Center​” segment, the company’s Energy Management segment was up over 9.6%.

Likewise, Vertiv reported first-quarter 2025 net sales of $2.04 million, up $397 million, or 24%, compared to the first quarter of 2024.

Schneider and Vertiv, according to Dell’Oro Group, are “virtually tied for global market share, separated by just a tenth of a percentage point.”

## Schneider Electric (France) and Vertiv (US) are Leading Players in the Data Centre PDU Market

The data center PDU market is estimated to be valued at USD 4.89 billion in 2026 and USD 14.23 billion by 2032, registering a CAGR of 19.5% from 2026 to 2032. The increasing expansion of hyperscale and AI-driven data centers is driving demand for intelligent and high-density power distribution solutions. Modern PDUs are increasingly integrated with digital infrastructure management platforms, enabling capabilities such as real-time monitoring, remote management, outlet-level control, and energy optimization, supporting efficient and reliable operation across enterprise, colocation, and cloud data center environments.

Schneider Electric (France), Vertiv (US), Eaton (Ireland), Legrand (France), and ABB (Switzerland) are among the major players in the data center PDU market. Market participants are expanding their intelligent PDU portfolios and strengthening their market presence through strategies such as product launches, partnerships, and technology-focused investments.

the pricing sheets I'm getting are all over the place."}],"status":"corroborated","statusRationale":"The core mechanism — large operators consolidating rack-power purchasing into multi-year framework/supply-capacity agreements rather than discrete PDU line items — is directly evidenced by the Digital Realty–Schneider $373M SCA and supported by industry reports documenting vendor consolidation (Schneider/Vertiv/Eaton/ABB/Legrand top set). However, the SCA press language explicitly notes the model 'preserv[es] the flexibility needed for a dynamic, multi-vendor environment,' which partially tempers the 'structurally closed' framing: frameworks are not necessarily single-vendor lock-outs. The falsifier (a named CE operator confirming an open multi-bidder PDU RFQ in 2026) was not found; only generic colo pricing forum chatter surfaced, which is silent on procurement openness. Absence of a falsifier is not refutation, and the load-bearing premise holds.","evidence":[{"title":"Schneider Electric and Digital Realty Announce $373M Supply Capacity Agreement","sourceType":"industry-report","publisher":"Schneider Electric / PRNewswire","date":"2025-11-19","excerpt":"Digital Realty has signed a $373 million Supply Capacity Agreement (SCA) for UPS, Low Voltage Switchgear, and Pre-Fabricated Skids; the strategic shift to an SCA model provides guaranteed capacity while preserving flexibility for a dynamic, multi-vendor environment.","url":"https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/schneider-electric-and-digital-realty-announce-373m-supply-capacity-agreement-to-meet-rising-data-center-demand-302620427.html"},{"title":"Schneider Electric and Vertiv are Leading Players in the Data Centre PDU Market","sourceType":"industry-report","publisher":"MarketsandMarkets","date":null,"excerpt":"Schneider Electric, Vertiv, Eaton, Legrand, and ABB are among the major players in the data center PDU market, expanding intelligent PDU portfolios through partnerships and integrated infrastructure offerings.","url":"https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/ResearchInsight/data-center-pdu-companies.asp"},{"title":"Data Center Power - Market Share Analysis, Industry Trends & Statistics","sourceType":"industry-report","publisher":"Mordor Intelligence / GII Research","date":"2025-06-16","excerpt":"Consolidation among equipment suppliers is reshaping electrical infrastructure priorities as hyperscale capacity additions push operators toward integrated, higher-voltage power trains.","url":"https://www.giiresearch.com/report/moi1850125-data-center-power-market-share-analysis-industry.html"}],"revisedLikelihood":0.55,"revisionRationale":"The framework/SCA consolidation mechanism is well corroborated by the Digital Realty–Schneider deal and vendor-concentration reports, nudging likelihood up. But the same SCA text explicitly preserves a 'dynamic, multi-vendor environment,' indicating frameworks are procurement structures rather than absolute foreclosures — and the SCA covers UPS/switchgear/skids, not PDUs specifically. This limits the upward revision. The falsifier was not found, so no downward pressure. Net: modest increase from 0.50 to 0.55."}
